payload.input: "Ethan," she says, tasting his name, and Julian lowers the camera from his eye, watching his wife with an expression that suggests he recognizes this shift, this dangerous softening. "You don’t have to be scared of me. I’m going to take care of you, alright? I’m going to show you exactly how to make her feel good, and you’re going to do such a good job for me, aren’t you?"  Ethan nods, his lower lip catching between his teeth, and Vivian leans in, her mouth brushing his forehead in a kiss that lingers too long for familial comfort, her lips pressing into the skin there as she inhales the boy-smell of him—deodorant failing against nervous sweat, the cotton of his shirt, something uniquely childlike underneath that she wants to corrupt and preserve simultaneously. Her hand slides down his arm, covering his trembling fingers where they rest on Maya’s thigh, and she guides him, her palm hot and dry against his clammy skin, pressing his fingertips against the wet heat of Maya’s entrance.
